mnemonics: REFlexive, Symmetric, and Transitive = Equivalence Relation (Remember R-S-T)
keyPoints: An equivalence relation partitions a set into disjoint equivalence classes.
The equivalence class of an element 'a' is the set of all elements related to 'a'.
The quotient set is the set of all equivalence classes of the set.
commonMistakes: Assuming all relations that are symmetric and transitive are also reflexive (they might not be).
Confusing equivalence relations with partial orders (which are reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive).
